pi_items.source: https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/ and https://www.fns.usda.gov/ - both are official USDA websites
Task description line states: 'Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Menu Planning Resources'
Task description states: 'containing at least 5 bookmarks from your research'
Task description states: 'Create a JSON file at ~/Documents/weekly_menu_plan.json' which expands to /home/ga/Documents/weekly_menu_plan.json

Official USDA National School Lunch Program standards (https://www.fns.usda.gov/school-meals/nutrition-standards/nslp-meal-pattern): K-5 minimum is 550 kcal, 6-8 minimum is 600 kcal, 9-12 minimum is 750 kcal. The lowest minimum across all grades is 550 kcal. Task description correctly states '550-850 kcal (standard range for K-12)'.

Official USDA National School Lunch Program standards (https://www.fns.usda.gov/school-meals/nutrition-standards/nslp-meal-pattern): K-5 maximum is 650 kcal, 6-8 maximum is 700 kcal, 9-12 maximum is 850 kcal. The highest maximum across all grades is 850 kcal. Task description correctly states '550-850 kcal (standard range for K-12)'.
Task description JSON structure example shows these exact five components, and they align with USDA school meal pattern requirements

privileged_info_summary: The task involves researching USDA school meal nutrition standards using real government sources (fdc.nal.usda.gov and fns.usda.gov). According to official 2024 USDA National School Lunch Program standards, the correct daily calorie ranges by grade are: K-5: 550-650 kcal, 6-8: 600-700 kcal, 9-12: 750-850 kcal. The task description correctly specifies the 550-850 kcal range representing the full span across all grade groups. However, the metadata defines calorie_min as 400 kcal and calorie_max as 1000 kcal, both contradicting official USDA standards (minimum should be 550, maximum should be 850). The required components are main_dish, side, vegetable, fruit, and milk. The bookmark folder 'Menu Planning Resources' and minimum of 5 bookmarks are accurately specified in metadata.
